category: Biometric identification and categorisation of natural persons
examples: Real-time remote biometric identification in public spaces
Biometric categorisation inferring race, political opinions, religion
Post-remote biometric identification with law enforcement exceptions
notes: Real-time biometric ID in public spaces is PROHIBITED under Article 5 except for specific law enforcement purposes with judicial authorisation.

category: Management and operation of critical infrastructure
examples: AI safety components for electricity grid management
notes: Only AI used as a safety component qualifies. General monitoring tools may not.

examples: Automated exam scoring that determines access to education
Proctoring systems used to detect prohibited behaviour
Assessment of learning outcomes determining progression
notes: AI that merely supports teachers without making binding decisions may be LIMITED_RISK.

category: Employment, workers management and access to self-employment
examples: Automated CV screening and candidate shortlisting
Task allocation and monitoring of performance
notes: This is the most common Annex III category for HR-tech products.

category: Access to and enjoyment of essential private and public services and benefits
examples: Life and health insurance risk classification
notes: Covers both private (insurance, credit) and public (welfare, emergency) essential services.

examples: Crime pattern analysis for individual risk assessment
AI evaluation of evidence reliability in criminal proceedings
Profiling in the context of crime detection
notes: Any AI used by law enforcement to assess individuals or predict crime falls here.

category: Migration, asylum and border control management
examples: Visa and asylum application risk assessment
Detection of individuals at irregular border crossing
notes: Often intersects with Article 5 prohibitions on biometric surveillance.

category: Administration of justice and democratic processes
examples: AI to assist courts in researching and interpreting facts and law
AI used for judicial or administrative decision support
notes: Any AI touching judicial decisions or democratic participation is HIGH_RISK by default.
